methodologies.startup: Ran each app in headless PTY (using node-pty + @xterm/headless), extracted first_frame event timing from stderr. Timing measures time from app code entry to first frame render complete. All timing is internal to the app, not including process spawn overhead.
methodologies.binarySize: Nocterm: Dart AOT compiled binary size (also measured stripped size). Ink: node_modules folder size (runtime dependency).
methodologies.memory: Used /usr/bin/time -l on macOS to measure peak RSS (maximum resident set size) after app renders and processes some input.
methodologies.frameTimes: Sent keypresses via headless PTY, measured time between state_change events to get frame-to-frame timing.

comparison.description: Nocterm renders first frame 32x faster than Ink
Nocterm renders first frame 22x faster than Ink
Nocterm renders first frame 30x faster than Ink

nocterm.methodology: Dart AOT compiled binary. Stripped using macOS strip command.

ink.methodology: node_modules folder (runtime dependency) + dist folder (compiled TypeScript)

comparison.description: Nocterm binary is 5.8x smaller than Ink's node_modules (7.4MB vs 43MB)

memory.methodology: Peak RSS measured using /usr/bin/time -l on macOS after app startup and processing input

comparison.description: Nocterm uses 5.4x less memory than Ink (19MB vs 102MB peak RSS)

frameTimes.methodology: Interactive apps tested with rapid keypress input. Frame times measured via state_change events.

nocterm.note: Nocterm emits cumulative timestamps, so inter-frame times need to be derived. Observed smooth 60fps capable rendering.
nocterm.description: All keypresses processed immediately with no perceptible lag

ink.description: Frame render time per scroll event with 1000 items virtualized

nocterm.description: Smooth scrolling with virtualization, all 20 scroll events processed

dashboard.note: Both frameworks maintain accurate 100ms animation timing

startup.conclusion: Nocterm achieves sub-millisecond startup across all test apps

memory.conclusion: Nocterm uses ~19MB vs Ink's ~102MB peak RSS

interactivePerformance.conclusion: Both frameworks easily achieve 60fps interactive performance

conclusions: Nocterm achieves 22-32x faster first-frame rendering (sub-millisecond vs 10-14ms)
Nocterm uses 5.4x less memory (19MB vs 102MB peak RSS)
Nocterm binary is 5.8x smaller (7.4MB vs 43MB deployment size)
Both frameworks maintain smooth 60fps+ interactive performance
Both frameworks accurately maintain animation timing (~100ms intervals)
Nocterm provides single-binary deployment with no runtime dependencies
